name: Advance America
description_short: Payday and installment lender since 1997 offering same-day cash. Provides $100–$10,000 loans in-store or online across 23 states with no minimum credit score required.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest payday and short-term consumer lenders in the United States, founded in 1997 and headquartered in Greenville, South Carolina. The company has operated as a subsidiary of Grupo Elektra — a Mexican retail and financial conglomerate — since its 2012 acquisition. With roughly 750 physical store locations across 23 states and a parallel online lending presence in those same markets, Advance America has funded over 157 million loans across its 25-plus year history. It holds state lending licenses in all operating states and has earned a Great Place to Work certification along with multiple Newsweek Greatest Workplaces designations — employee-facing credentials, not consumer financial certifications.

Advance America's product lineup is built around high-cost, short-term credit for consumers who lack access to traditional bank lending. Payday loans range from $100 to $2,000 with APRs typically between 350% and 700%, structured as flat fees of roughly $10–$30 per $100 borrowed. Installment loans span $500 to $4,000 with APRs from 200% to 348% or higher — a $650 South Carolina installment loan repaid over six months, for instance, carries approximately a 348% APR and a total repayment cost of around $1,341. Title loans secured by a vehicle go up to $10,000, and revolving lines of credit reach up to $4,000. Approval is based on income and employment rather than credit history, making these products accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit.

What distinguishes Advance America operationally is speed and physical availability. In-store customers can receive cash in as little as 30 minutes after approval, while online applicants approved before noon typically receive same-day or next-business-day funding. The company runs a customer rewards program that accumulates points for on-time payments, redeemable toward fee discounts on future loans — a feature uncommon among payday lenders. Its BBB institutional rating stands at A+ with accreditation granted in 2024, and complaint volume has declined to roughly 74 in the past year, down from a prior annual average of approximately 114.

The critical caveat for consumers is cost. APRs on Advance America's products are extremely high by mainstream lending standards — payday loan rates routinely exceed 400%, and even their multi-month installment loans carry rates that most financial advisors would classify as predatory. Consumer review scores on BBB average just 1.7 out of 5 from 154 reviews, a significant gap from the institutional A+ accreditation grade. Advance America is best treated as a last-resort option for borrowers who genuinely cannot access credit unions, CDFIs, or personal loan lenders — not a cost-effective long-term borrowing solution. The fees are disclosed and the company is legally licensed, but the total cost of borrowing substantially exceeds nearly all alternatives available to creditworthy consumers.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. Borrowers repay loan principal plus fees per the signed loan agreement.

tiers.name: Payday Loans
features: In-store cash disbursement in as little as 30 minutes
No minimum credit score — income and employment-based approval
Available in-store and online in 23 states

tiers.name: Installment Loans
features: APR range 200%–348%+ depending on state and term
Example: $650 over 6 months in SC carries ~348% APR, ~$1,341 total repayment

tiers.name: Line of Credit
features: Borrow, repay, and reborrow as needed

pros: Founded in 1997 — 25+ years in operation with over 157 million loans funded
Approximately 750 physical store locations in 23 states with in-store cash available in as little as 30 minutes
Approval based on income and employment — no minimum credit score required
Customer rewards program awards points for on-time payments, redeemable for fee discounts on future loans
A+ BBB rating with accreditation since 2024 and declining complaint trend (~74 complaints in past year)
Online applicants approved before noon typically receive same-day or next-business-day funding
Newsweek Greatest Workplaces certified across four categories in 2024–2025
cons: Extremely high APRs: payday loans run 350%–700%, installment loans 200%–348%+, far above mainstream lending
BBB consumer review score is only 1.7 out of 5 from 154 reviews — a sharp gap from the A+ institutional grade
Available in only 23 states — residents of other states have no access to their products
Owned since 2012 by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican retail conglomerate — not a U.S.-based independent consumer lender
No confirmed dedicated mobile app — online account management is limited to a web portal
best_for: Consumers with bad or no credit who need same-day cash and cannot qualify for traditional bank or credit union loans
Underbanked individuals facing short-term financial emergencies such as medical bills, car repairs, or utility shutoffs
Borrowers who need physical in-store cash pickup within 30 minutes of approval
People who have been declined elsewhere and need a regulated, licensed short-term loan option as a last resort
diagnosis: Advance America is genuinely best suited for consumers with poor or no credit who face an urgent, short-term cash shortage and have already been turned away by lower-cost options like credit unions, employer advances, or personal loan lenders. The primary caveat is steep cost — APRs of 350%–700% on payday products make these loans among the most expensive legal credit available, and a 1.7/5 consumer review score on BBB suggests real borrower dissatisfaction. This is a last-resort tool, not a routine borrowing solution.
services: Customer Rewards Program (points redeemable for fee discounts)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
